source.title: Tonsillectomy for recurrent tonsillitis
source.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international commissioning and clinical evidence requiring UAE ENT adaptation
source.population: Adults and children with recurrent severe episodes of sore throat due to acute tonsillitis; exclusions and specialist conditions are explicit.
source.setting: Primary and ENT assessment of recurrent tonsillitis and shared-decision documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for episode history, documented clinically significant sore throats, functional impact, complications, shared decision, and distinguishing recurrent tonsillitis from sleep apnoea, suspected cancer, quinsy, and emergency presentations. It does not generate a tonsillectomy recommendation.

exact_location.heading: Summary and recommendation — recurrent episodes, documentation, and functional impact

exact_location.heading: Summary and recommendation — recurrent episodes, documentation, and functional impact
exact_location.locator: official guidance Summary and Recommendation sections

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting recurrent sore-throat episodes attributed by a clinician to acute tonsillitis, episode history, normal-function impact, work or school impact, and complications without applying an automatic procedural threshold.

items.rationale: The exact EBI section supports episode history, onset and recurrence pattern, symptom and functional impact, associated tonsillitis features, relevant negatives, concerns, and clinician-performed ENT or general assessment without applying a procedural threshold.

items.final_wording: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.

source.title: Tonsillectomy for recurrent tonsillitis
source.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international commissioning and clinical evidence requiring UAE ENT adaptation
source.population: Adults and children with recurrent severe episodes of sore throat due to acute tonsillitis; exclusions and specialist conditions are explicit.
source.setting: Primary and ENT assessment of recurrent tonsillitis and shared-decision documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for episode history, documented clinically significant sore throats, functional impact, complications, shared decision, and distinguishing recurrent tonsillitis from sleep apnoea, suspected cancer, quinsy, and emergency presentations. It does not generate a tonsillectomy recommendation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation and exclusions — shared decision and separate pathways
exact_location.locator: official guidance Recommendation and exclusion list

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.

items.rationale: The exact shared-decision and exclusions section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending tonsillectomy or setting a threshold.

items.final_wording: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis (Sore Throat)
source.population: Adult and pediatric patients with sore throat.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth sore-throat documentation; partial for general ENT settings and diagnosis-specific treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.

items.rationale: The exact DHA sore-throat features and testing section was reviewed for UAE acute-episode context, but no diagnostic score or automatic test interpretation was mapped.

items.final_wording: Lists airway, swallowing, breathing, pain, immune compromise, neck stiffness, dehydration, and complication prompts for escalation.

items.rationale: The exact DHA red-flag section was reviewed for acute safety context; no automatic escalation or referral text was mapped.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting recurrent sore-throat episodes attributed by a clinician to acute tonsillitis, episode history, normal-function impact, work or school impact, and complications without applying an automatic procedural threshold.

source.title: Tonsillectomy for recurrent tonsillitis
source.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international commissioning and clinical evidence requiring UAE ENT adaptation
source.population: Adults and children with recurrent severe episodes of sore throat due to acute tonsillitis; exclusions and specialist conditions are explicit.
source.setting: Primary and ENT assessment of recurrent tonsillitis and shared-decision documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for episode history, documented clinically significant sore throats, functional impact, complications, shared decision, and distinguishing recurrent tonsillitis from sleep apnoea, suspected cancer, quinsy, and emergency presentations. It does not generate a tonsillectomy recommendation.

exact_location.heading: Summary and recommendation — recurrent episodes, documentation, and functional impact
exact_location.locator: official guidance Summary and Recommendation sections

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting recurrent sore-throat episodes attributed by a clinician to acute tonsillitis, episode history, normal-function impact, work or school impact, and complications without applying an automatic procedural threshold.

added_items.rationale: The exact EBI section supports episode history, onset and recurrence pattern, symptom and functional impact, associated tonsillitis features, relevant negatives, concerns, and clinician-performed ENT or general assessment without applying a procedural threshold.

added_items.final_wording: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.

source.title: Tonsillectomy for recurrent tonsillitis
source.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international commissioning and clinical evidence requiring UAE ENT adaptation
source.population: Adults and children with recurrent severe episodes of sore throat due to acute tonsillitis; exclusions and specialist conditions are explicit.
source.setting: Primary and ENT assessment of recurrent tonsillitis and shared-decision documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for episode history, documented clinically significant sore throats, functional impact, complications, shared decision, and distinguishing recurrent tonsillitis from sleep apnoea, suspected cancer, quinsy, and emergency presentations. It does not generate a tonsillectomy recommendation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation and exclusions — shared decision and separate pathways
exact_location.locator: official guidance Recommendation and exclusion list

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.

added_items.rationale: The exact shared-decision and exclusions section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending tonsillectomy or setting a threshold.

added_items.final_wording: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis (Sore Throat)
source.population: Adult and pediatric patients with sore throat.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth sore-throat documentation; partial for general ENT settings and diagnosis-specific treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.

added_items.rationale: The exact DHA sore-throat features and testing section was reviewed for UAE acute-episode context, but no diagnostic score or automatic test interpretation was mapped.

added_items.final_wording: Lists airway, swallowing, breathing, pain, immune compromise, neck stiffness, dehydration, and complication prompts for escalation.
